text: sprint 47 planning is thursday 10am. reminder of the process: PMs bring ranked candidates, eng brings capacity, we commit as a team — nothing enters the sprint after commit without swapping something out
can we actually enforce the no-mid-sprint-additions rule this time :upside_down_face:
the rule IS the enforcement — anyone can say no to additions by pointing at it. escalations go through me, not directly to engineers
capacity note: lucy is on dependency-update rotation and chris is still ramping, so plan for ~80%
noted. carry-overs from 46: the rate-limit dashboard (ACME-254) and export-UX fix (ACME-273)
254 is 90% done, just needs review. don't re-estimate it as fresh work
good call, marking it as carry-over-finish. see everyone thursday
customer feedback theme: our API rate limits confuse people. 3 of the top-10 support themes are rate-limit related. proposal: per-endpoint limits with a live usage dashboard
per-endpoint is fairer but way more surface area. today it's one global bucket per key (documented on the Confluence rate limiting page)
the legacy plans make it worse — old accounts have half the limits and no visibility. that's most of the confused tickets honestly
so maybe phase 1 is just the dashboard (visibility) and legacy-plan migration, phase 2 is per-endpoint?
+1, dashboard is a week of work, per-endpoint limiter is a quarter. don't boil the ocean
support strongly endorses the dashboard, we'd deflect a ticket a day minimum
phase 1 it is — writing the spec today, tickets under ACME-254 epic
i'll take the dashboard UI, been wanting an excuse to touch the usage charts
